title: Workbook — Baptism
html:
Student Workbook

For use after class or in a group. Print this page or work in your notebook.

Remembering the Essentials

  1. What does the word «Baptism» mean, and why is it called the «gateway to the sacraments»?
  2. Name at least three prefigurations of Baptism in the Old Testament and explain what each one symbolizes.
  3. What are the matter and form of the sacrament of Baptism?
  4. Who can be the minister of Baptism? Under what circumstances can someone who is not a bishop, priest, or deacon baptize?
  5. List the five principal effects of Baptism according to the Catechism.

For Reflection and Writing

Answer each question honestly. There are no «correct» answers to these personal questions.

1. Do you know the exact date of your Baptism? Do you know the name of the church, the priest, and your godparents?

If you do not know, find out this week. Being aware of your Baptism is like remembering your spiritual birthday. Write down what you discover.

 

 

2. Baptism makes us children of God, temples of the Holy Spirit, and members of the Church. Which of these three truths do you find hardest to believe about yourself? Why?

Sometimes it is hard to believe God loves us that much. Sometimes we do not feel part of the Church. Or perhaps we forget that God dwells within us.

 

 

3. St. Paul says that in Baptism we were «buried with Christ» so that we might «walk in newness of life» (Rom 6:4). What sin, habit, or attitude do you need to bury so that new life can spring forth?

Be honest. This is not about feeling guilty but about identifying what keeps grace from growing in you.

 

 

4. The baptismal character can never be erased. You belong to Christ forever. How does knowing you bear that indelible mark change the way you live?

If God does not regret having made you his own, how can you doubt his love?

 

 

5. How can you live out your baptismal priesthood this week in a concrete way?

Offer your work, forgive someone, pray for others, give witness. Choose one action and write it down.

 

 

Practical Activities

A. Read Romans 6:3-11 aloud

St. Paul describes Baptism as death and resurrection with Christ. Read the passage slowly, twice. The first time just listen. The second time, underline every word that speaks of death and every word that speaks of life. What do you discover?

 

 

 

 

B. Renew your baptismal promises

Find the text of the baptismal promises (in the Missal or online). Kneel down and answer each question aloud. Take your time with the renunciations: «Do you renounce Satan? Do you renounce sin? Do you renounce the lure of evil?» Then profess the faith calmly. Which promise was hardest for you to say?

 

 

 

C. Visit the baptismal font in your parish

Approach the baptismal font. Touch the water or simply look at the place where you were baptized (or where baptisms take place in your parish). Pray a Hail Mary in thanksgiving for the gift of Baptism. If you can, light a candle for your godparents.

 

 

 

Memory Verse

«We were buried therefore with him by baptism into death, so that as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, we too might walk in newness of life.»

— Romans 6:4

Prayer for This Week

Lord Jesus, who sanctified the waters of the Jordan
and commanded your Church to baptize all nations,
renew in me the grace of my Baptism.
I was born again of water and the Spirit;
help me to live as a child of God,
to renounce sin and to follow you faithfully.
May I never forget the dignity you have given me
and may my life reflect the newness of life
that began in the waters of Baptism.
Thank you for the indelible seal of your love.
Amen.

Print Workbook
